title: Neottia smallii
taxonomy: Neottia smallii is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is placed within the subfamily Orchidoideae and tribe Cranichideae. Additionally, it is classified in the genus Neottia, which is a group of orchids commonly known as the "bee orchids" due to their resemblance to bees. Neottia smallii is native to the southeastern United States, where it grows in woodland environments. The exact classification of Neottia smallii may be subject to revision as new research and discoveries are made in the field of botany.
uses: Neottia smallii, also known as the Appalachian ladies' tresses, has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various properties. Its roots have been used in herbal remedies to treat a range of conditions. intro: Neottia smallii, also known as the Small's ladies' tresses or Small's orchid, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Neottia smallii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including diabetes, hypertension, and digestive issues.
